data.title: Cantor Fitzgerald Adjusts Price Target for Strategy Amid Bitcoin Market Dynamics
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, U.S.-based financial firm Cantor Fitzgerald has revised its price target for Michael Saylor's Bitcoin-focused company, Strategy, while maintaining a positive outlook on the cryptocurrency's long-term potential. The firm has downplayed concerns about forced liquidation, despite the Financial Times reporting a significant reduction in the 12-month price target for Strategy stock. Cantor Fitzgerald has reportedly lowered the target by 60%, adjusting it to $229 from the previous $560, as noted in a Thursday analyst report.

Cantor's "buy" rating for Strategy remains unchanged, with analysts asserting that fears of forced liquidations are "not warranted," despite the attention these concerns have received. The analysts highlighted that Strategy possesses "enough cash" to cover dividend payments for 21 months and can raise additional funds through equity facilities if necessary. They emphasized that unless Bitcoin experiences a 90% drop from current levels, the fear of forced liquidation is unwarranted.

Despite the revised price target, Strategy's stock has underperformed compared to Cantor's previous expectations. At the time of writing, Strategy's shares were trading around $186, marking a 27% decline over the past month and a 35% decrease year-to-date, according to Google Finance data. Cantor Fitzgerald is the ninth-largest shareholder in the company.

Strategy's stock faces short-term challenges, including the potential removal from the MSCI Index due to digital asset holdings exceeding 50% of total assets. This scenario could lead to "forced selling of MSTR," but Cantor views this as a "somewhat warranted" concern that presents a "near-term flow headwind." Nonetheless, Cantor remains optimistic about Strategy and Bitcoin's price trajectory, describing the current market pullback as a "healthy" correction. They believe Bitcoin is on track to surpass gold's market capitalization.

Cantor's analysts expressed confidence that Bitcoin could eventually overtake gold's market cap, noting that Bitcoin's current market cap is just 6.1% of gold's. To achieve this, Bitcoin would need to reach a price of $1,577,860. Other analysts have also predicted Bitcoin's potential to eclipse gold's market capitalization, with forecasts suggesting Bitcoin could surpass $1.8 million by 2035. However, Bitcoin's price would need to increase nearly 16-fold to exceed the value of the world's largest precious metal. Since the beginning of 2025, gold's price has risen 58%, outperforming Bitcoin's 1.5% year-to-date decline, according to TradingView data.

data.title: Binance Futures to Delist Four Perpetual Contracts in December
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform's Futures division is set to close all positions and conduct an automatic settlement on USDⓈ-M SKATEUSDT, REIUSDT, FISUSDT, and VOXELUSDT Perpetual Contracts at 2025-12-10 09:00 (UTC). Following the settlement, these contracts will be delisted. Users are advised to close any open positions before the delisting time to avoid automatic settlement. Starting from 2025-12-10 08:30 (UTC), new positions for these contracts will not be permitted.

During the final hour leading up to the scheduled settlement time, the Futures Insurance Fund will not be utilized to support the liquidation process for these contracts. Any liquidation triggered during this period will be executed as a single Immediate or Cancel order (IOCO), which will be offloaded into the market in one attempt. If the IOCO execution leaves sufficient assets in the user's account to meet the required Maintenance Margin, the liquidation will cease. However, if the IOCO fails to fully reduce the position to satisfy Margin Maintenance requirements, any unfilled portion will be resolved through the Auto-Deleveraging (ADL) process. Users are strongly encouraged to monitor and manage open positions during this final hour due to potential heightened volatility and reduced liquidity.

To protect users and mitigate risks in extremely volatile market conditions, Binance Futures may implement additional protective measures for these contracts without further notice. These measures could include adjusting maximum leverage values, position values, and maintenance margins in each margin tier, updating funding rates, changing price index constituents, and using the Last Price Protected mechanism to update the Mark Price. Users should remain vigilant and informed about these potential changes to ensure effective management of their positions.

data.title: ZKsync Introduces Interoperability Feature with Atlas Upgrade
data.description: According to Foresight News, ZKsync has announced the activation of its interoperability feature through the Atlas upgrade. This development allows all ZK chains to interact natively with ZKsync, enabling any ZK Stack chain to access Ethereum's DeFi ecosystem. ZKsync stated that when combined with Prividiums, this mechanism will offer institutions a private system architecture with direct access to public market liquidity.

data.title: Ethereum Options Traders Target $6,500 as Open Interest Surges to Record Levels
data.description:
Ethereum traders are aggressively positioning for upside, with Deribit data showing a massive build-up in open interest around the $6,500 strike. According to the latest figures, open interest tied to these calls has now surpassed $380 million, making it the single largest concentration among all ETH option strikes on the platform.

data.title: Russia to Prioritize Cryptocurrency Legislation in Upcoming Year
data.description:
According to PANews, Anatoly Aksakov, Chairman of the Financial Markets Committee of Russia's State Duma, has announced that cryptocurrency and stablecoin regulation will be a legislative priority for Russia next year. As the country moves to regulate its crypto sector, establishing a legal framework for digital finance is deemed essential. Aksakov noted that the Russian Central Bank plans to implement comprehensive cryptocurrency regulations by 2026.

Historically, Russian regulators have opposed unrestricted cryptocurrency trading within the national economy. However, this week, they signaled a willingness to ease restrictions on crypto circulation. Currently, crypto assets and derivatives can only be traded within a highly limited "experimental legal regime" by a select group of privileged market participants, including foreign trade enterprises, financial companies, and "highly qualified" investors. Russian monetary authorities are in discussions with the Ministry of Finance to broaden investor access and regulate transactions outside the "experimental legal regime." Previously, authorities indicated that banks would be permitted to engage in digital currency-related activities and that funds could invest in crypto-based derivatives.
